title: Code implementation task #4: Write secure validation function

features: JSON syntax validation with detailed error messages
Recursive structure validation with path tracking
Key name validation with forbidden key detection
SQL injection pattern detection (union, select, drop, etc.)
XSS injection detection (script tags, event handlers)

test_categories: Basic Validation (valid objects, arrays, nested structures)
Invalid JSON (syntax errors, incomplete JSON, wrong quotes)
Security Tests (SQL injection, XSS, event handlers, command injection)
Depth Limits (reject excessive nesting, allow reasonable nesting)
Size Limits (reject oversized payloads, accept reasonable sizes)
Key Validation (valid/invalid key names with special characters)
Circular References (detect and allow non-circular objects)
Schema Validation (required fields, type checking, constraints)
Real-world Scenarios (API requests, webhooks, malicious payloads)

security_analysis.validation_approach: Multi-layer defense with syntax validation → structure validation → security pattern detection

basic_validation.description: Validate JSON with default security settings
basic_validation.code: const { validate } = require('./src/json_validator'); const result = validate('{"name": "Alice"}'); if (result.valid) { console.log(result.data); } else { console.log('Errors:', result.errors); }

custom_limits.description: Create validator with custom depth/size limits
custom_limits.code: const { createValidator } = require('./src/json_validator'); const validator = createValidator({ maxDepth: 10, maxPayloadSize: 1024 * 1024 }); const result = validator.validate(jsonString);

schema_validation.code: const { validateWithSchema } = require('./src/json_validator'); const schema = { type: 'object', required: ['email'], properties: { email: { type: 'string', format: 'email' } } }; const result = validateWithSchema({ email: 'test@example.com' }, schema);

notes: All 31 tests passing (100% success rate). Validator prevents JSON injection attacks, prototype pollution, billion laughs attacks, and command injection. Configurable constraints allow per-use-case tuning. Suitable for API request validation, webhook processing, and untrusted JSON sources.
